main_ingredients: Subway cookie (chocolate chip or other variety)

serving.description: 1 meal (6-inch Italian B.M.T. sub + 1 Subway cookie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 6-inch Subway Italian B.M.T. sub (approximately 255 g) plus one Subway cookie (approximately 45–48 g), totalling approximately 300–305 g per meal.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sub and Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie — USDA Branded Food / Subway published nutrition data cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central branded entries
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived by combining USDA FoodData Central branded entries for Subway 6-inch Italian B.M.T. sub (default vegetables, no sauce) and a Subway chocolate chip cookie. Per-100g values are calculated from the combined meal weight of approximately 305 g. The Italian B.M.T. sub alone is approximately 252–260 g per USDA branded data; the cookie is approximately 45 g. Combined total ~305 g used as serving basis. Values represent a typical default preparation without added sauces.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based mixed meals — white wheat bread sub roll analogue; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Italian B.M.T. Cookie Meal as a composite. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) white wheat sub roll GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database; (2) processed deli meats and vegetables contribute negligible glycemic load; (3) cookie (chocolate chip, wheat-based) GI ~55–58 per Sydney GI Database analogues; (4) composite meal GI weighted toward ~55 due to protein and fat content of the meats moderating postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 97.6) / 100 ≈ 53.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~1,891 mg per meal serving) from processed deli meats (salami, pepperoni, ham) and pickled vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to its very high sodium content (~1,891 mg per serving), which represents approximately 82% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg; if consumed, no additional high-sodium foods should be eaten that day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load from white wheat sub roll and cookie, with estimated glycemic load of ~53.7 per meal
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its high glycemic load (~53.7 per serving) driven by the refined white wheat bread roll and the cookie; choosing a wholegrain bread option and omitting the cookie would substantially reduce postprandial glucose impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat from processed meats (salami, pepperoni), and trans fat presence
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is of high concern for cardiovascular disease due to its combination of very high sodium (~1,891 mg per serving), saturated fat from processed deli meats, and trace trans fats; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and consider lower-sodium, lower-saturated-fat alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Italian B.M.T. Cookie Meal is a fast-food combo meal sold at Subway restaurants in Singapore, consisting of a 6-inch Italian B.M.T. submarine sandwich paired with one Subway cookie (typically chocolate chip or another available variety). The Italian B.M.T. — where B.M.T. stands for Biggest, Meatiest, Tastiest — is one of Subway's signature sandwiches, featuring three types of Italian-style processed deli meats: Genoa salami, pepperoni, and ham, layered on a freshly baked Italian white wheat bread roll. The sandwich is typically assembled with standard vegetable toppings including lettuce, tomato, onion, green pepper, olives, and pickles, and may be customised with sauces. The bread roll is soft with a slightly chewy crust, and the meats provide a salty, savoury, and mildly spiced flavour profile. The accompanying Subway cookie is a soft-baked, sweet biscuit approximately 45 g in weight. Nutritionally, this meal is energy-dense at approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with a high sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g, approximately 1,891 mg per full meal serving) primarily from the cured and processed deli meats. The meal provides moderate protein (~12.5 g per 100 g) and a significant carbohydrate load (~32 g per 100 g) from the refined wheat bread and cookie. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low) for the composite meal, but the glycemic load per serving is high (~53.7) due to the large carbohydrate quantity. This meal is of high clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, primarily due to its very high sodium content and refined carbohydrate load, leading to a high glycemic load. In Singapore, this meal is available at Subway outlets on field survey and is priced at approximately SGD 13.60.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in an approved primary source for the Subway Italian B.M.T. Cookie Meal as a composite Singapore field survey product. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded entries for the Subway 6-inch Italian B.M.T. sub and a Subway chocolate chip cookie, combined and recalculated per 100 g. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white wheat bread and cookie analogues. Confidence is medium due to the adapted composite calculation and the absence of a direct Singapore-specific nutritional analysis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are calculated by combining USDA FoodData Central branded data for the Subway 6-inch Italian B.M.T. sub (default vegetables, no sauce, ~260 g) and a Subway chocolate chip cookie (~45 g), yielding a combined meal weight of ~305 g. Per-100g values are derived from this combined weight. The serving.size_g is set to 305g for consistency. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entries for these products. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entries for these products. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entries for these products. 5. GI is an analogous estimate at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white wheat bread sub rolls and cookie analogues; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as 53.7. 6. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA branded data for the component foods. 7. The halal status of Subway Singapore varies by outlet; the Funan outlet listed in the source data should be verified independently for current halal certification. 8. The input food_group was listed as 'Desserts' in the seed record, which appears to be a misclassification; this record has been reclassified to 'Mixed' as the primary component is a sandwich meal combo. 9. Price data (SGD 13.60) is preserved from the field survey data source record.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that food_group 'Desserts' from seed record was a misclassification; corrected to 'Mixed'.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback for q4_glycemic_completeness. Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55. Recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving to 53.7 based on a consistent serving size of 305g and carbohydrate content. Updated serving.size_g to 305g for consistency with nutrient source notes. Updated gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes, and llm_training.natural_language_description to reflect the corrected GI category and GL value. Verified aliases_by_language consistency — all entries in aliases_by_language confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases list. Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified GI is numeric > 0. Verified ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Confirmed PASS on all hard gates and Q1–Q8.
